Hi. I guess… this week was pretty rough at work. During one of our meetings, I felt like everyone was laughing at me after I spoke. It just made me really uncomfortable and like maybe I wasn’t any good at all. I’ve been keeping quiet since then because I didn’t want to risk making the same mistake again. It’s hard not to feel like I’m just not measuring up.  
Yeah, I know it could be just how things are sometimes, but it still feels like they’re judging me unfairly. I don’t know, maybe I’m just overthinking it, but it’s hard to shake off. I don’t really see why they’d laugh if I wasn’t good enough. Maybe I’m just not cut out for this kind of thing.  
I’m sorry if this sounds like I’m complaining, but it’s just… frustrating. I don’t want to seem like I’m making a big deal out of nothing, but this keeps bothering

Hi. I guess... the meeting didn’t go so well for me again. I tried to say something, but it felt like no one really wanted to hear it. I’m not sure if it’s just how things are or if maybe I’m just not good enough. It’s hard to explain.  
Yeah, I know it sounds kind of silly, but sometimes I wonder if I’m just overthinking it or if it’s all in my head. I mean, why should my opinion matter more than anyone else’s? It’s like I’m always invisible, you know?  
I don’t know, maybe I should just keep quiet next time. It’s easier that way. Honestly, I’m not sure if talking would change anything anyway. Maybe I’m just better off staying out of the spotlight.  
Yeah, it’s frustrating, but what can you do? Sometimes it just feels like no matter what you do, you’re going to get knocked down or ignored.

Hi. I guess… this week was pretty rough. That meeting where my manager criticized my report really stuck with me. I kept thinking maybe I’m just not cut out for this job after all. I stayed late trying to fix everything, but it felt like no matter what I did, it wasn’t enough. Honestly, I’m just tired and scared that maybe I’m really failing everyone. It’s hard not to feel like I’m just a burden on the team. I don’t know, maybe I’m just being too hard on myself. But it’s frustrating because I want to prove myself, and it feels like no one believes me. Sometimes I wonder if I’m just not good enough. I guess that’s why I’ve been avoiding talking to my manager since then. It’s easier to keep quiet than face another criticism. I hope this therapy helps, but I’m not sure if I’m doing it right. Maybe I’m just the type of person who needs more

Maria, a 28-year-old writer, submitted several chapters of her first novel to a writing group and received critical feedback that she interpreted as confirmation of her underlying belief, "I'm not talented," causing her to procrastinate and doubt her future in writing.

This situation triggered her underlying belief that she is not competent enough, intensifying her fear of being exposed as inadequate in her role.

Client recently discovered that their partner has been frequently omitting details about their whereabouts and spending significant time with a new coworker. This situation has activated the client's negative core belief of being unlovable, leading them to feel increasingly anxious and suspicious in the relationship.

During a family dinner, Emily attempts to share her recent professional achievements, but her parents quickly shift the conversation to her brother's new business venture, barely acknowledging her success. This situation activates Emily's negative core belief that she is less valued and less important than her brother.

Hi. Yeah, so last week when I tried to buy something online, my credit card got declined. I thought maybe I messed up the payment or something, but then I realized it was because there wasn’t enough money in my account. It just made me feel really... ashamed. I don’t know, maybe I’m just bad with numbers or something. But honestly, it’s frustrating because no matter what I do, it seems like this will never change. I guess I just don’t want anyone to find out how much trouble I’m in. It’s easier to keep it hidden.

(quietly) I hope you understand

Hi. I guess... this week was kind of rough at work. During a meeting, I suggested something, but it was just like, brushed off immediately. I felt like maybe I’m just not good enough or something. It’s hard not to think that maybe I’m just messing things up again. I don’t know, it’s frustrating. Maybe I just shouldn’t try so much next time. Yeah, I guess that’s about it. I’m not sure if it’s really worth talking about. Maybe I’ll just keep quiet next time.

Hi. I guess... this week has been pretty rough. I keep feeling like my partner is ignoring me on social media, and honestly, it just makes me feel invisible. I can’t stop checking his posts, but it doesn’t seem to help. It’s like no matter what I do, I’m still just not enough in his life. How do I stop feeling so lost? I don’t know, maybe I’m just overthinking it. But it feels like I’m always the one who misses the mark.  
I’m not sure if talking about this will change anything, but I wanted to bring it up. Sometimes I wonder if I’m just too sensitive or something. Maybe I should just let it go and move on. But then again, it’s hard not to feel hurt when it feels like he’s ignoring me. What do you think?  
It’s frustrating, but I don’t know how else to handle it. I’ve tried asking him about it

It just felt like I wasn’t good enough anymore. I don’t know, it’s hard to shake off these thoughts. I’m not sure if it’s even worth trying to keep things stable between us. Maybe I should just move on. But that feels selfish too. So, what do you think? Should I try to get more involved or just step back? I just feel so lost.

Hi. I guess... the seminar didn’t go so well for me. I felt kind of lost most of the time, especially when we started talking about these new coding things. Everyone else seemed to get it pretty fast, and I just kept thinking maybe I’m just not cut out for this. It was embarrassing, honestly. I’m not sure if it’s worth trying again next time. It just felt like everyone else was moving ahead and I was stuck behind. Maybe I’m just not good at this. I don’t know. Sometimes I wonder if I’m just not smart enough. That’s what keeps running through my head.

Hi. I guess... last week was pretty rough. At the family dinner, my mom said some things about me working and taking care of my kids, and it just... really hurt. I don’t

Yeah, I guess that’s all. I’m not sure if this helps much. Sometimes it just feels like talking about these things is pointless.  
(Shakes head) I don’t know... maybe I’m just unlucky or something. Everyone else"

Hi. I guess... the family gathering was kind of tough for me. Everyone was asking about why I’m not back at work, and it just felt like they were all judging me. I tried to keep smiling, but inside I felt really anxious and ashamed. What can I say? It’s hard to explain.

I don’t know, maybe it’s just how things have always been. Maybe I shouldn’t even try to change anything. After all, I have my kids, and that’s what matters most. It’s just easier that way.  
Yeah, I suppose. But sometimes it feels like I’m just stuck, like I can’t get ahead or prove myself
